card.intuition: When a mismatch occurs during string matching, don't start over — use the failure function to jump back only as far as needed based on the overlapping prefix-suffix structure already matched.
card.analogy: You're typing a password and mistype the last character. Instead of clearing the whole field, you know the prefix you typed is still a valid suffix of the pattern — jump back to the latest safe restart point.
card.time_complexity: O(n + m) — O(m) to build failure function, O(n) to search
when_to_use: Guaranteed O(n + m) single-pattern search (vs Rabin-Karp's O(nm) worst case)
When you need to count all non-overlapping or overlapping occurrences of a pattern
Checking if a string is a rotation of another (search in doubled string)
Finding the shortest period of a string: period = m - lps[m-1]
gotchas: The failure function (LPS array) encodes the length of the longest proper prefix that is also a suffix at each position — build it carefully before searching
When a mismatch occurs at pattern[j], set j = lps[j-1] (not j-1 and not j) — this is the critical non-obvious step
Overlapping matches: after a match, set j = lps[j-1] instead of 0 to allow the next match to reuse the suffix

exercise.prompt: Build the KMP failure function (LPS array): lps[i] = length of longest proper prefix of pattern[0..i] that is also a suffix.
hints: When there's a mismatch and length > 0, fall back to lps[length - 1] — this is the longest prefix-suffix of the prefix-suffix we were trying to extend
Do NOT decrement i here — we're trying to match pattern[i] against a shorter prefix of pattern, so keep i fixed and try a smaller length
exercise.test_code:
assert compute_lps('abcabc')    == [0,0,0,1,2,3], 'abcabc LPS'
assert compute_lps('aabaaab')   == [0,1,0,1,2,2,3], 'aabaaab LPS'
assert compute_lps('aaaa')      == [0,1,2,3],        'All same'
assert compute_lps('abcde')     == [0,0,0,0,0],      'No repeats'
assert compute_lps('aabaa')     == [0,1,0,1,2],      'aabaa LPS'
assert compute_lps('a')         == [0],               'Single char'
print('PASS')
